name: Arganda del Rey, Campo Real, Vía Verde del Tajuña – circular route
name_original: Arganda del Rey, Campo Real, Vía Verde del Tajuña  – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Arganda Del Rey traverse a diverse landscape within the Community of Madrid, including parts of the Southeast Regional Park. The region is characterized by the river valleys of the Jarama, Manzanares, and Tajuña, which contribute to fertile plains and varied riparian vegetation. Cyclists encounter a mix of agricultural areas, Mediterranean forests, scrubland, pine forests, and holm oak stands, with some routes featuring gypsum-rich slopes. The terrain offers both flat, well-paved sections and routes with moderate to significant elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Arganda Del Rey The most popular road cycling route is Arganda del Rey, Campo Real, Vía Verde del Tajuña – circular route, a 46.3 miles (74.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route offers a comprehensive tour through varied landscapes, including sections of the Tajuña Greenway. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Marañosa Climb – Titulcia Main Square loop from La Poveda, a moderate 43.2 miles (69.6 km) path. This route includes a notable climb and scenic views as it loops through the area. Local road cyclists also love the Tajuña Greenway – Plaza del Pueblo de Loeches loop from Arganda del Rey, a 42.8 miles (68.9 km) trail leading through the Tajuña Greenway and fertile plains, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Arganda Del Rey is defined by river valleys, agricultural areas, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat greenways to routes with significant elevation. The routes in Arganda Del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Arganda Del Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes can I find around Arganda del Rey?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes available around Arganda del Rey, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult. This includes options through the Southeast Regional Park and along river valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Arganda del Rey?
A: The region offers a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find flat, well-paved sections, especially along the Vía Verde del Tajuña, as well as routes with moderate to significant elevation gains. The landscape includes agricultural areas, Mediterranean forests, scrubland, pine forests, and unique gypsum-rich slopes.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Arganda del Rey?
A: Yes, Arganda del Rey offers several easy routes, with 4 specifically categorized as such. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33993943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway loop from La Poveda&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 38.2 km route that utilizes the generally flat and well-paved Vía Verde del Tajuña, making it a good option for those seeking a less challenging ride. The Vía Verde itself is ideal for families due to its gentle gradients.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes or climbs in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marañosa Climb – Plaza Mayor de Chinchón loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains, with nearly 950 meters of ascent over 96.2 km. The region features climbs such as La Marañosa, providing a good test for experienced cyclists.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes traverse the Parque Regional del Sureste, offering views of diverse ecosystems. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2620629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5123913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarama Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2864570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;confluence of the Jarama and Manzanares rivers&lt;/a&gt; from the Cortados de Rivas. The river valleys of the Jarama, Manzanares, and Tajuña also provide scenic riparian landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the cycling paths?
A: Yes, cyclists can discover various points of interest. These include the old railway line of the Vía Verde del Tajuña, the historic iron bridge over the Jarama River, and the remains of the Castle of Perales. The Museo del Ferrocarril de Arganda, La Poveda is also a notable stop for those interested in local history.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Arganda del Rey?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977041797/arganda-del-rey-campo-real-via-verde-del-tajuna-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arganda del Rey, Campo Real, Vía Verde del Tajuña – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 74.5 km and offers a comprehensive tour of varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Arganda del Rey?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler, wetter days.

Q: How accessible is Arganda del Rey for cyclists using public transport?
A: Arganda del Rey is well-connected by public transport, including Metro Line 9 from Madrid. This makes it convenient for cyclists to reach the town and start their rides without needing a car, especially for those coming from the capital.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Arganda del Rey?
A: The road cycling routes in Arganda del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic river valleys, and the well-maintained sections of the Vía Verde del Tajuña.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes, bars, and restaurants. The Vía Verde del Tajuña, in particular, connects several municipalities, offering opportunities for refreshment stops along the way.

Q: Are there any specific routes that include the Vía Verde del Tajuña?
A: Absolutely. The Vía Verde del Tajuña is a highlight of the region. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38294102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway – Plaza del Pueblo de Loeches loop from Arganda del Rey&lt;/a&gt; integrate sections of this popular greenway, providing a smooth and scenic ride through fertile plains.
tips: Fantastic way to cycle, without worrying about cars. Very far from the road, it allows you to enjoy the countryside. Very well paved, perfect for riding a road bike.

Villar del Olmo Town Hall has a QR code system, and you can discover the 21 points of interest, both scenic and historical, through your mobile or tablet, with information and videos from a drone view. There are many routes and many places of interest.

Arganda has relevant historical monuments, for example the Church of San Juan Bautista, built at the end of the 17th century, with its characteristic 57-meter tower. The Casa del Rey also stands out, a small palace from the end of the 16th century.

Church of San Juan Bautista, Arganda del Rey
